1	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	"""
3	Distributed multi-tier storage manager for MP mode
4	"""
5	
6	# Standard
7	from contextlib import contextmanager
8	from typing import Iterator, Literal, Optional
9	import threading
10	import time
11	
12	# First Party
13	from lmcache.logging import init_logger
14	from lmcache.native_storage_ops import Bitmap, PeriodicEventNotifier
15	from lmcache.v1.distributed.api import (
16	    DEFAULT_ATTN_WINDOW_DESC,
17	    AttnWindowDesc,
18	    MemoryLayoutDesc,
19	    ObjectKey,
20	    PrefetchHandle,
21	    PrefetchMode,
22	    TrimPolicy,
23	)
24	from lmcache.v1.distributed.config import EvictionConfig, StorageManagerConfig
25	from lmcache.v1.distributed.error import L1Error, strerror
26	from lmcache.v1.distributed.internal_api import L1MemoryDesc, L2AdapterListener
27	from lmcache.v1.distributed.l1_manager import L1Manager
28	from lmcache.v1.distributed.l2_adapters import create_l2_adapter
29	from lmcache.v1.distributed.l2_adapters.base import L2AdapterInterface
30	from lmcache.v1.distributed.l2_adapters.config import L2AdapterConfigBase
31	from lmcache.v1.distributed.l2_adapters.reconfiguration import (
32	    L2ReconfigurableAdapter,
33	    L2ReconfigureError,
34	)
35	from lmcache.v1.distributed.l2_adapters.serde_wrapper import SerdeL2AdapterWrapper
36	from lmcache.v1.distributed.quota_manager import QuotaManager
37	from lmcache.v1.distributed.serde import create_serde_processor
38	from lmcache.v1.distributed.storage_controllers import (
39	    L1EvictionController,
40	    L2AdapterEvictionState,
41	    L2EvictionController,
42	    PrefetchController,
43	    StoreController,
44	)
45	from lmcache.v1.distributed.storage_controllers.prefetch_policy import (
46	    create_prefetch_policy,
47	)
48	from lmcache.v1.distributed.storage_controllers.store_policy import (
49	    AdapterDescriptor,
50	    create_store_policy,
51	)
52	from lmcache.v1.memory_management import MemoryObj
53	from lmcache.v1.mp_observability.errors import LMCacheTimeoutError
54	from lmcache.v1.mp_observability.event import Event, EventType
55	from lmcache.v1.mp_observability.event_bus import get_event_bus
56	from lmcache.v1.mp_observability.otel_init import register_gauge
57	from lmcache.v1.mp_observability.trace.decorator import (
58	    enable_tracing,
59	    is_tracing_enabled,
60	    publish_call_event,
61	)
62	from lmcache.v1.platform import HAS_EVENTFD
63	
64	logger = init_logger(__name__)
65	
66	
67	class StorageManager:
68	    def __init__(self, config: StorageManagerConfig):
69	        self._l1_manager = L1Manager(config.l1_manager_config)
70	        self._event_bus = get_event_bus()
71	
72	        # L1 eviction controller
73	        self._eviction_controller = L1EvictionController(
74	            l1_manager=self._l1_manager,
75	            eviction_config=config.eviction_config,
76	        )
77	        self._eviction_controller.start()
78	
79	        # L2 adapters and store controller. When an adapter config carries
80	        # a ``serde_config``, the adapter is wrapped with
81	        # ``SerdeL2AdapterWrapper`` so controllers see a plain L2 adapter
82	        # and serde is transparent.
83	        self._l1_memory_desc = self._l1_manager.get_l1_memory_desc()
84	        self._next_adapter_id = 0
85	        # Serializes add_l2_adapter / delete_l2_adapter against each other.
86	        self._lifecycle_lock = threading.Lock()
87	        # Guards the _l2_adapters and _adapter_descriptors dicts.
88	        self._adapters_lock = threading.Lock()
89	        self._registered_l2_listeners: list[L2AdapterListener] = []
90	        self._l2_adapters: dict[int, L2AdapterInterface] = {}
91	        self._adapter_descriptors: dict[int, AdapterDescriptor] = {}
92	        for ac in config.l2_adapter_config.adapters:
93	            adapter_id, adapter, descriptor = self._build_l2_adapter(ac)
94	            self._l2_adapters[adapter_id] = adapter
95	            self._adapter_descriptors[adapter_id] = descriptor
96	
97	        PeriodicEventNotifier.create(
98	            interval_ms=config.periodic_notifier_interval_ms,
99	            use_eventfd=HAS_EVENTFD,
100	        )
101	
102	        # Per-cache_salt quota registry. Shared across the L2 eviction
103	        # controller (reads quotas each cycle) and the HTTP quota
104	        # endpoints (CRUD). Present even when no adapter uses
105	        # IsolatedLRU so the HTTP layer has a stable ``quota_manager``
106	        # reference. No explicit cleanup on close — the registry is
107	        # just a dict protected by a lock and has no OS resources.
108	        self._quota_manager = QuotaManager()
109	
110	        # Unified L2 eviction controller for all adapters with eviction
111	        # config. Aggregate-usage policies (``LRU``, ``noop``) need
112	        # ``max_capacity_bytes > 0`` to compute a usage fraction;
113	        # adapters without capacity are skipped for those. Isolated
114	        # policies (``IsolatedLRU``) operate on per cache_salt byte
115	        # counts which the base class tracks regardless of capacity,
116	        # so they are wired up unconditionally.
117	        l2_eviction_states: list[L2AdapterEvictionState] = []
118	        for adapter_id, ac in zip(
119	            self._l2_adapters, config.l2_adapter_config.adapters, strict=True
120	        ):
121	            adapter = self._l2_adapters[adapter_id]
122	            if self._should_enable_l2_eviction(adapter, ac.eviction_config):
123	                assert ac.eviction_config is not None  # make linter happy
124	                l2_eviction_states.append(
125	                    L2AdapterEvictionState(
126	                        adapter_id=adapter_id,
127	                        adapter=adapter,
128	                        eviction_config=ac.eviction_config,
129	                    )
130	                )
131	        self._l2_eviction_controller = L2EvictionController(
132	            l2_eviction_states, quota_manager=self._quota_manager
133	        )
134	        self._l2_eviction_controller.start()
135	
136	        # Controllers receive the initial set as ordered lists; they key
137	        # their own copies by ``descriptor.index`` (== adapter_id) and learn
138	        # of later changes via add_adapter/request_remove_adapter.
139	        self._store_controller = StoreController(
140	            l1_manager=self._l1_manager,
141	            l2_adapters=list(self._l2_adapters.values()),
142	            adapter_descriptors=list(self._adapter_descriptors.values()),
143	            policy=create_store_policy(config.store_policy),
144	        )
145	        self._store_controller.start()
146	
147	        # Prefetch controller
148	        self._prefetch_controller = PrefetchController(
149	            l1_manager=self._l1_manager,
150	            l2_adapters=list(self._l2_adapters.values()),
151	            adapter_descriptors=list(self._adapter_descriptors.values()),
152	            policy=create_prefetch_policy(config.prefetch_policy),
153	            max_in_flight=config.prefetch_max_in_flight,
154	        )
155	        self._prefetch_controller.start()
156	
157	        # L2 usage gauge — one observation per adapter, tagged by
158	        # ``l2_name``.  Parallel to L1Manager's ``l1_memory_usage_bytes``.
159	        register_gauge(
160	            "lmcache.l2",
161	            "lmcache_mp.l2_usage_bytes",
162	            (
163	                "Bytes currently held in each L2 adapter, tagged by "
164	                "``l2_name`` (one observation per adapter)."
165	            ),
166	            self.get_l2_usages,
167	        )
168	
169	    # External APIs for serving engine integration code to call
170	    @enable_tracing()
171	    def reserve_write(
172	        self,
173	        keys: list[ObjectKey],
174	        layout_desc: MemoryLayoutDesc,
175	        mode: Literal["new", "update", "all"],
176	    ) -> dict[ObjectKey, MemoryObj]:
177	        """
178	        Reserve the object for writing into the storage manager.
179	
180	        Args:
181	            keys (list[ObjectKey]): List of object keys to reserve for writing.
182	            layout_desc (MemoryLayoutDesc): Description of the memory layout
183	                for the objects to be reserved.
184	            mode (Literal["new", "update", "all"]): Reservation mode.
185	            - "new": Reserve only new objects that do not exist.
186	            - "update": Reserve only existing objects for update.
187	            - "all": Reserve all writable objects regardless of existence.
188	
189	        Returns:
190	            dict[ObjectKey, MemoryObj]: A dictionary mapping object keys to their
191	                reserved memory objects. Note that not all requested keys could be
192	                reserved (e.g., out of memory or write conflict)
193	        """
194	        reserve_result = self._l1_manager.reserve_write(
195	            keys=keys,
196	            is_temporary=[False] * len(keys),
197	            layout_desc=layout_desc,
198	            mode=mode,
199	        )
200	
201	        result = {k: m for k, (e, m) in reserve_result.items() if m is not None}
202	        successful_keys = list(result.keys())
203	        failed_keys = [k for k, (e, m) in reserve_result.items() if m is None]
204	        self._event_bus.publish(
205	            Event(
206	                event_type=EventType.SM_WRITE_RESERVED,
207	                metadata={
208	                    "succeeded_keys": successful_keys,
209	                    "failed_keys": failed_keys,
210	                },
211	            )
212	        )
213	
214	        oom_keys = [
215	            k for k, (e, _) in reserve_result.items() if e == L1Error.OUT_OF_MEMORY
216	        ]
217	        if oom_keys:
218	            self._event_bus.publish(
219	                Event(
220	                    event_type=EventType.L1_ALLOCATION_FAILED,
221	                    metadata={"during": "l1_store", "keys": oom_keys},
222	                )
223	            )
224	
225	        return result
226	
227	    @enable_tracing()
228	    def finish_write(
229	        self,
230	        keys: list[ObjectKey],
231	    ) -> None:
232	        """
233	        Finish writing the objects into the storage manager.
234	
235	        Args:
236	            keys (list[ObjectKey]): List of object keys that have been written.
237	        """
238	        finish_result = self._l1_manager.finish_write(keys)
239	        successful_keys = [k for k, e in finish_result.items() if e == L1Error.SUCCESS]
240	        failed_keys = [k for k, e in finish_result.items() if e != L1Error.SUCCESS]
241	        self._event_bus.publish(
242	            Event(
243	                event_type=EventType.SM_WRITE_FINISHED,
244	                metadata={
245	                    "succeeded_keys": successful_keys,
246	                    "failed_keys": failed_keys,
247	                },
248	            )
249	        )

253	    @contextmanager
254	    def read_prefetched_results(
255	        self,
256	        keys: list[ObjectKey],
257	    ) -> Iterator[list[MemoryObj] | None]:
258	        """
259	        Read the memory objects from L1 storage that has been prefetched beforehand.
260	        Yielding an optional list of memory objects corresponding to the requested
261	        keys. If any the object is not found in L1, None is yielded.
262	
263	        Args:
264	            keys (list[ObjectKey]): List of object keys to reserve for reading.
265	
266	        Returns:
267	            Iterator[list[MemoryObj] | None]: An iterator yielding an optional list of
268	                memory objects corresponding to the requested keys.
269	
270	        Note:
271	            If any object is not found in L1 storage, None is yielded. In this case,
272	            this function will release release the read lock of all successfully read
273	            memory objects when exiting the context.
274	
275	            If the caller raised exception during the processing of the yielded memory
276	            objects, this function will ensure that the read locks will be decreased.
277	        """
278	        # Manual TRACE_CALL emission for the context manager.  The
279	        # ``@enable_tracing`` decorator cannot wrap a ``@contextmanager``
280	        # generator function (it would publish the call to the wrapper
281	        # rather than to ``__enter__``).  Emit enter/exit events
282	        # directly, gated on the tracing flag for zero overhead when
283	        # disabled.
284	        if is_tracing_enabled():
285	            publish_call_event(
286	                "lmcache.v1.distributed.storage_manager."
287	                "StorageManager.read_prefetched_results.__enter__",
288	                {"keys": keys},
289	            )
290	        read_results = self._l1_manager.unsafe_read(keys)
291	        good_keys: list[ObjectKey] = []
292	        good_objs: list[MemoryObj] = []
293	        bad_keys: list[ObjectKey] = []
294	        not_found_keys: list[ObjectKey] = []
295	        write_locked_keys: list[ObjectKey] = []
296	        all_good = True
297	        for k, (e, o) in read_results.items():
298	            if o is None:
299	                logger.error(
300	                    "Failed to read prefetched object %s from L1 storage: %s",
301	                    k,
302	                    strerror(e),
303	                )
304	                bad_keys.append(k)
305	                all_good = False
306	                if e == L1Error.KEY_NOT_EXIST:
307	                    not_found_keys.append(k)
308	                elif e == L1Error.KEY_NOT_READABLE:
309	                    write_locked_keys.append(k)
310	                continue
311	
312	            good_keys.append(k)
313	            good_objs.append(o)
314	
315	        # L1 read-failure anomaly reporting: unsafe_read is required to be
316	        # called post-reserve_read, so any failure here is a lock/eviction
317	        # race, not a normal cache miss.
318	        if not_found_keys:
319	            self._event_bus.publish(
320	                Event(
321	                    event_type=EventType.L1_READ_FAILED,
322	                    metadata={
323	                        "during": "l1_retrieve",
324	                        "reason": "not_found",
325	                        "keys": not_found_keys,
326	                    },
327	                )
328	            )
329	        if write_locked_keys:
330	            self._event_bus.publish(
331	                Event(
332	                    event_type=EventType.L1_READ_FAILED,
333	                    metadata={
334	                        "during": "l1_retrieve",
335	                        "reason": "write_locked",
336	                        "keys": write_locked_keys,
337	                    },
338	                )
339	            )
340	
341	        successfully_yielded = False
342	
343	        try:
344	            yield good_objs if all_good else None
345	            successfully_yielded = True
346	        except Exception:
347	            logger.exception(
348	                "Exception occurred while processing read prefetched results",
349	            )
350	            raise
351	        finally:
352	            # Decrease the read lock for all successfully read memory objects
353	            # if None is yielded or exception occurs during caller's processing
354	            if not all_good or not successfully_yielded:
355	                self._l1_manager.finish_read(good_keys)
356	                self._event_bus.publish(
357	                    Event(
358	                        event_type=EventType.SM_READ_PREFETCHED_FINISHED,
359	                        metadata={
360	                            "succeeded_keys": good_keys,
361	                            "failed_keys": bad_keys,
362	                        },
363	                    )
364	                )
365	            if is_tracing_enabled():
366	                publish_call_event(
367	                    "lmcache.v1.distributed.storage_manager."
368	                    "StorageManager.read_prefetched_results.__exit__",
369	                    {"keys": keys},
370	                )
371	
372	    @enable_tracing()
373	    def finish_read_prefetched(
374	        self,
375	        keys: list[ObjectKey],
376	        extra_count: int = 0,
377	    ) -> None:
378	        """Finish reading prefetched objects.
379	
380	        Args:
381	            keys: Object keys that have been read.
382	            extra_count: Extra read locks to release per key
383	                (on top of the default 1).
384	        """
385	        finish_result = self._l1_manager.finish_read(keys, extra_count=extra_count)
386	        successful_keys = [k for k, e in finish_result.items() if e == L1Error.SUCCESS]
387	        failed_keys = [k for k, e in finish_result.items() if e != L1Error.SUCCESS]
388	        self._event_bus.publish(
389	            Event(
390	                event_type=EventType.SM_READ_PREFETCHED_FINISHED,
391	                metadata={
392	                    "succeeded_keys": successful_keys,
393	                    "failed_keys": failed_keys,
394	                },
395	            )
396	        )
397	
398	    @enable_tracing()
399	    def submit_prefetch_task(
400	        self,
401	        keys: list[ObjectKey],
402	        layout_desc: MemoryLayoutDesc,
403	        extra_count: int = 0,
404	        external_request_id: str = "",
405	        policy: TrimPolicy = TrimPolicy.PREFIX,
406	        attn_desc: AttnWindowDesc = DEFAULT_ATTN_WINDOW_DESC,
407	        skip_l2: bool = False,
408	        mode: PrefetchMode = PrefetchMode.LOOKUP,
409	    ) -> PrefetchHandle:
410	        """Prefetch objects into L1 asynchronously.
411	
412	        Args:
413	            keys: Object keys to prefetch.
414	            layout_desc: Memory layout description.
415	            extra_count: Extra workers (on top of the default
416	                1) that will independently retrieve the same
417	                key.  Total locks = 1 + extra_count.
418	            external_request_id: Request ID from the caller
419	                for end-to-end log tracing.
420	            policy: Which retained-subset policy to apply (see
421	                :class:`TrimPolicy`).  ``PREFIX`` keeps the contiguous prefix;
422	                ``SPARSE`` keeps every found key (gap-tolerant).
423	            attn_desc: Cross-chunk attention windows of all object groups, in
424	                object-group order.
425	            skip_l2: If True, do not load from L2. For ``LOOKUP`` only
426	                already-resident L1 keys are returned; for ``WARM`` nothing is
427	                loaded and an empty handle is returned.
428	            mode: The prefetch intent (see :class:`PrefetchMode`).  ``WARM``
429	                retains loaded keys and pins none; ``LOOKUP`` (default) pins
430	                them for an imminent reader and follows the policy.
431	
432	        Returns:
433	            PrefetchHandle to track the task.
434	        """
435	        if mode is PrefetchMode.WARM:
436	            # Warm path: load all keys, pin none. skip_l2 makes it a no-op.
437	            prefetch_request_id = -1
438	            if not skip_l2 and keys and self._l2_adapters:
439	                prefetch_request_id = self._prefetch_controller.submit_prefetch_request(
440	                    keys,
441	                    layout_desc,
442	                    extra_count=extra_count,
443	                    policy=policy,
444	                    mode=mode,
445	                )
446	            return PrefetchHandle(
447	                prefetch_request_id=prefetch_request_id,
448	                external_request_id=external_request_id,
449	                l1_found_indices=(),
450	                total_requested_keys=len(keys),
451	                submit_time=time.monotonic(),
452	                l2_orig_indices=(
453	                    tuple(range(len(keys))) if prefetch_request_id != -1 else ()
454	                ),
455	            )
456	
457	        # NOTE: now we only have L1, so the prefetch is essentially checking how many
458	        # objects are already in L1, and adding read locks to them.
459	
460	        l1_read_result = self._l1_manager.reserve_read(keys, extra_count=extra_count)
461	
462	        if policy is TrimPolicy.SPARSE:
463	            # SPARSE: retain a read lock on every L1 hit (not just the leading
464	            # prefix) and send all L1 misses to L2 as one coalesced request.
465	            # reserve_read locks only SUCCESS keys, so the found-set already
466	            # equals the locked set -- nothing to release.
467	            l1_found_indices: list[int] = []
468	            succeeded_keys: list[ObjectKey] = []
469	            sparse_l2_indices: list[int] = []
470	            remaining_keys: list[ObjectKey] = []
471	            for i, key in enumerate(keys):
472	                ent = l1_read_result.get(key)
473	                if ent is not None and ent[0] == L1Error.SUCCESS and ent[1] is not None:
474	                    l1_found_indices.append(i)
475	                    succeeded_keys.append(key)
476	                else:
477	                    sparse_l2_indices.append(i)
478	                    remaining_keys.append(key)
479	
480	            self._event_bus.publish(
481	                Event(
482	                    event_type=EventType.SM_READ_PREFETCHED,
483	                    metadata={
484	                        "succeeded_keys": succeeded_keys,
485	                        "failed_keys": remaining_keys,
486	                    },
487	                )
488	            )
489	
490	            prefetch_request_id = -1
491	            if remaining_keys and self._has_l2_adapters():
492	                prefetch_request_id = self._prefetch_controller.submit_prefetch_request(
493	                    remaining_keys,
494	                    layout_desc,
495	                    extra_count=extra_count,
496	                    policy=TrimPolicy.SPARSE,
497	                    attn_desc=attn_desc,
498	                    mode=mode,
499	                )
500	            return PrefetchHandle(
501	                prefetch_request_id=prefetch_request_id,
502	                external_request_id=external_request_id,
503	                l1_found_indices=tuple(l1_found_indices),
504	                total_requested_keys=len(keys),
505	                submit_time=time.monotonic(),
506	                l2_orig_indices=tuple(sparse_l2_indices),
507	            )
508	
509	        hit_count = 0
510	        for key in keys:
511	            entry = l1_read_result.get(key, None)
512	            if entry is None:
513	                break
514	
515	            err, obj = entry
516	            if err != L1Error.SUCCESS:
517	                break
518	
519	            hit_count += 1
520	
521	        # NOTE: For L1, there will be cases that "object in the middle" is not found.
522	        # In this case, we need to `finish_read` for the latter objects so that
523	        # there won't be dangling read locks.
524	        skipped_keys = []
525	        for key in keys[hit_count:]:
526	            if key in l1_read_result and l1_read_result[key][1] is not None:
527	                # this key is actually reserved, need to release the read lock
528	                skipped_keys.append(key)
529	
530	        if skipped_keys:
531	            self._l1_manager.finish_read(skipped_keys, extra_count=extra_count)
532	
533	        self._event_bus.publish(
534	            Event(
535	                event_type=EventType.SM_READ_PREFETCHED,
536	                metadata={
537	                    "succeeded_keys": keys[:hit_count],
538	                    "failed_keys": keys[hit_count:],
539	                },
540	            )
541	        )
542	
543	        if skip_l2:
544	            return PrefetchHandle(
545	                prefetch_request_id=-1,
546	                external_request_id=external_request_id,
547	                l1_found_indices=tuple(range(hit_count)),
548	                total_requested_keys=len(keys),
549	                submit_time=time.monotonic(),
550	                l2_orig_indices=(),
551	            )
552	
553	        # Submit remaining keys to L2 prefetch controller
554	        remaining_keys = keys[hit_count:]
555	        prefetch_request_id = -1
556	        l2_orig_indices: tuple[int, ...] = ()
557	        if remaining_keys and self._has_l2_adapters():
558	            prefetch_request_id = self._prefetch_controller.submit_prefetch_request(
559	                remaining_keys,
560	                layout_desc,
561	                extra_count=extra_count,
562	                attn_desc=attn_desc,
563	                policy=policy,
564	                mode=mode,
565	            )
566	            # The controller indexes its result bitmap over remaining_keys
567	            # (0-based); map those local indices back to original positions.
568	            l2_orig_indices = tuple(range(hit_count, len(keys)))
569	
570	        submit_time = time.monotonic()
571	        logger.debug(
572	            "Prefetch request submitted: "
573	            "%d total keys, %d L1 prefix hits, "
574	            "%d remaining for L2 "
575	            "(external_request_id=%s, "
576	            "prefetch_request_id=%d)",
577	            len(keys),
578	            hit_count,
579	            len(remaining_keys),
580	            external_request_id,
581	            prefetch_request_id,
582	        )
583	
584	        return PrefetchHandle(
585	            prefetch_request_id=prefetch_request_id,
586	            external_request_id=external_request_id,
587	            l1_found_indices=tuple(range(hit_count)),
588	            total_requested_keys=len(keys),
589	            submit_time=submit_time,
590	            l2_orig_indices=l2_orig_indices,
591	        )
592	
593	    def _combine_found(
594	        self, handle: PrefetchHandle, l2_local: "Bitmap | None"
595	    ) -> Bitmap:
596	        """Merge the L1 found indices with an L2 result bitmap into one bitmap
597	        over the original key positions.
598	
599	        ``l2_local`` is indexed over the keys submitted to L2 (0-based); its
600	        set bits are mapped back to original positions via
601	        ``handle.l2_orig_indices``.
602	        """
603	        found = Bitmap(handle.total_requested_keys)
604	        found.batched_set(handle.l1_found_indices)
605	        if l2_local is not None:
606	            # gather maps each L2 set bit i to its original position
607	            # ``l2_orig_indices[i]``; batched_set drops any position >= size.
608	            found.batched_set(l2_local.gather(handle.l2_orig_indices))
609	        return found
610	
611	    def query_prefetch_lookup_hits(
612	        self,
613	        handle: PrefetchHandle,
614	    ) -> int | None:
615	        """
616	        Query the number of prefix-hit chunks for a prefetch task before the
617	        L2 prefetching is done.
618	
619	        Args:
620	            handle (PrefetchHandle): The handle of the lookup task.
621	
622	        Returns:
623	            the number of prefix-hit chunks (L1 + L2) if the lookup is done,
624	            None if it's still in progress or the prefetch task is already done.
625	
626	        Note:
627	            This function is designed for the scenario where the caller wants
628	            to check the L1 prefix hits as soon as possible without waiting for
629	            the whole prefetch task to be done.
630	            When the prefetch task is already done and the prefetch task result
631	            has already been queried by `query_prefetch_status`, this function
632	            will return None forever for the same prefetch handle.
633	            Therefore, it's the caller’s responsibility to make sure not calling
634	            this function after the prefetch task is done.
635	        """
636	        # Prefix-path only: l1_found_indices is contiguous, so len() == prefix hits.
637	        l1_hits = len(handle.l1_found_indices)
638	        if handle.prefetch_request_id == -1:
639	            # No L2 request: the L1 prefix hit count is final.
640	            return l1_hits
641	
642	        l2_r = self._prefetch_controller.query_lookup_result(handle.prefetch_request_id)
643	        if l2_r is None:
644	            # Still in progress, or already consumed by query_prefetch_status.
645	            return None
646	        # L2 lookup done: total prefix hits are L1 plus the L2 continuation.
647	        return l1_hits + l2_r
648	
649	    def wait_prefetch_status(
650	        self,
651	        handle: PrefetchHandle,
652	        timeout: float,
653	    ) -> bool:
654	        """
655	        Block until the prefetch task for ``handle`` has a result, or timeout.
656	
657	        L1-only prefetches (``prefetch_request_id == -1``) have no L2 result to
658	        wait for and return immediately. This lets a caller avoid busy-polling
659	        query_prefetch_status; the status itself is still retrieved via
660	        query_prefetch_status afterwards.
661	
662	        Args:
663	            handle (PrefetchHandle): The handle of the prefetch task.
664	            timeout: Maximum number of seconds to wait for the L2 result.
665	
666	        Returns:
667	            True if a result is available within the timeout (always True for
668	            an L1-only prefetch), False if the wait timed out.
669	        """
670	        if handle.prefetch_request_id == -1:
671	            return True
672	        return self._prefetch_controller.wait_prefetch_result(
673	            handle.prefetch_request_id, timeout
674	        )
675	
676	    def query_prefetch_status(
677	        self,
678	        handle: PrefetchHandle,
679	    ) -> Bitmap | None:
680	        """
681	        Query the status of the prefetch task.
682	
683	        Args:
684	            handle (PrefetchHandle): The handle of the prefetch task.
685	
686	        Returns:
687	            the found-key bitmap (over original positions) if the prefetch is
688	            done, None if it's still in progress. Derive the prefix hit count
689	            via ``count_leading_ones``.
690	        """
691	        l2_r: Bitmap | None = None
692	        if handle.prefetch_request_id != -1:
693	            l2_r = self._prefetch_controller.query_prefetch_result(
694	                handle.prefetch_request_id
695	            )
696	            if l2_r is None:
697	                return None
698	
699	        found = self._combine_found(handle, l2_r)
700	        # popcount (not count_leading_ones) so the log is accurate for
701	        # non-contiguous policies (SEGMENTED_PREFIX / SPARSE) too.
702	        total_hits = found.popcount()
703	        elapsed_ms = (time.monotonic() - handle.submit_time) * 1000
704	
705	        if total_hits > 0:
706	            # L1 and L2 sets are disjoint (only L1-misses go to L2).
707	            l1_hits = len(handle.l1_found_indices)
708	            l2_hits = l2_r.popcount() if l2_r is not None else 0
709	            logger.info(
710	                "Prefetch request completed (L1+L2): "
711	                "%d/%d retained keys (%d L1, %d L2) in %.1f ms "
712	                "(external_request_id=%s, prefetch_request_id=%d)",
713	                total_hits,
714	                handle.total_requested_keys,
715	                l1_hits,
716	                l2_hits,
717	                elapsed_ms,
718	                handle.external_request_id,
719	                handle.prefetch_request_id,
720	            )
721	        return found
722	
723	    def touch_l1_keys(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
724	        """
725	        Touch the keys in L1 storage, marking the keys
726	        as accessed(retrieved or stored).
727	
728	        Args:
729	            keys (list[ObjectKey]): List of object keys to touch.
730	        """
731	        self._l1_manager.touch_keys(keys)
732	
733	    def unsafe_read(
734	        self, keys: list[ObjectKey]
735	    ) -> tuple[list[ObjectKey], list[MemoryObj]]:
736	        """Read already read-locked objects without acquiring new read locks."""
737	        read_results = self._l1_manager.unsafe_read(keys)
738	        good_keys: list[ObjectKey] = []
739	        good_objs: list[MemoryObj] = []
740	        for key in keys:
741	            err, obj = read_results.get(key, (L1Error.KEY_NOT_EXIST, None))
742	            if err != L1Error.SUCCESS or obj is None:
743	                continue
744	            good_keys.append(key)
745	            good_objs.append(obj)
746	        return good_keys, good_objs
747	
748	    @property
749	    def quota_manager(self) -> QuotaManager:
750	        """Per-cache_salt quota registry.
751	
752	        Exposed so the HTTP layer can serve CRUD endpoints without
753	        reaching into private state. Always non-``None`` — the
754	        storage manager creates the registry at construction time.
755	        """
756	        return self._quota_manager
757	
758	    @property
759	    def l1_memory_desc(self) -> L1MemoryDesc:
760	        """Descriptor of the L1 memory buffer backing this storage manager."""
761	        return self._l1_memory_desc
762	
763	    def get_l2_usages(
764	        self,
765	    ) -> list[tuple[int | float, dict[str, object]]]:
766	        """Per-adapter L2 usage in OTel-observation shape.
767	
768	        Backing data for the ``lmcache_mp.l2_usage_bytes`` observable
769	        gauge.  One entry per configured adapter.
770	
771	        Returns:
772	            A list of ``(total_bytes_used, {"l2_name": <type_name>})``
773	            tuples — empty when no L2 adapters are configured.  Adapters
774	            whose ``get_usage()`` raises are skipped (the gauge prefers
775	            silence over a poison observation).
776	        """
777	        out: list[tuple[int | float, dict[str, object]]] = []
778	        for _adapter_id, desc, adapter in self._snapshot_adapters():
779	            try:
780	                usage = adapter.get_usage()
781	            except Exception:
782	                logger.exception(
783	                    "L2 adapter %s get_usage() failed; skipping in gauge",
784	                    desc.type_name,
785	                )
786	                continue
787	            out.append((int(usage.total_bytes_used), {"l2_name": desc.type_name}))
788	        return out
789	
790	    def get_usage_bytes_by_cache_salt(self) -> dict[str, int]:
791	        """Aggregate ``cache_salt`` byte usage across every L2 adapter.
792	
793	        Used by the HTTP quota endpoints to report ``current_usage_gb``
794	        alongside the configured limit. Aggregation is a simple sum:
795	        each adapter tracks the same salt independently so the totals
796	        are additive.
797	        """
798	        totals: dict[str, int] = {}
799	        for _adapter_id, _desc, adapter in self._snapshot_adapters():
800	            snap = adapter.get_usage().bytes_by_cache_salt
801	            for salt, used in snap.items():
802	                totals[salt] = totals.get(salt, 0) + used
803	        return totals
804	
805	    # L2 APIs
806	    def get_l2_adapter_reconfigure_status(self) -> dict:
807	        """Return status for all runtime-reconfigurable L2 adapters.
808	
809	        Returns:
810	            JSON-serializable status. If no reconfigurable adapter is configured,
811	            ``enabled`` is ``False`` and the adapter list is empty.
812	        """
813	        type_names = {
814	            adapter_id: desc.type_name
815	            for adapter_id, desc, _ in self._snapshot_adapters()
816	        }
817	        adapters = []
818	        for adapter_index, (
819	            l2_adapter_index,
820	            adapter,
821	        ) in enumerate(self._list_reconfigurable_l2_adapters()):
822	            status = dict(adapter.reconfigure_status())
823	            if l2_adapter_index in type_names:
824	                status["backend"] = type_names[l2_adapter_index]
825	            status["adapter_index"] = adapter_index
826	            status["l2_adapter_index"] = l2_adapter_index
827	            adapters.append(status)
828	
829	        return {
830	            "enabled": bool(adapters),
831	            "num_adapters": len(adapters),
832	            "adapters": adapters,
833	        }
834	
835	    def reconfigurable_l2_backends(self) -> set[str]:
836	        """Return the ``type_name`` of every L2 adapter that supports runtime
837	        reconfiguration.
838	
839	        Returns:
840	            The set of reconfigurable adapter ``type_name`` strings (empty when
841	            none are reconfigurable). The ``{backend}`` path parameter the
842	            ``/reconfigure`` routes expect is the adapter's ``type_name``.
843	        """
844	        return {
845	            desc.type_name
846	            for _adapter_id, desc, adapter in self._snapshot_adapters()
847	            if self._unwrap_reconfigurable_l2_adapter(adapter) is not None
848	        }
849	
850	    def reconfigure_l2_adapter(
851	        self,
852	        adapter_index: int,
853	        operation: str,
854	        payload: dict[str, object],
855	    ) -> dict:
856	        """Route a runtime reconfiguration request to one L2 adapter.
857	
858	        Args:
859	            adapter_index: Zero-based reconfigurable-adapter index.
860	            operation: Adapter-specific operation name.
861	            payload: Adapter-specific operation payload.
862	
863	        Returns:
864	            JSON-serializable operation result.
865	        """
866	        adapter = self._get_reconfigurable_l2_adapter(adapter_index)
867	        result = adapter.reconfigure(operation, payload)
868	        result["adapter_index"] = adapter_index
869	        return result
870	
871	    def add_l2_adapter(self, config: L2AdapterConfigBase) -> int:
872	        """Blocking function to add a new L2 adapter at runtime. Thread-safe.
873	
874	        Args:
875	            config: The adapter configuration.
876	
877	        Returns:
878	            The stable id assigned to the new adapter.
879	        """
880	        with self._lifecycle_lock:
881	            adapter_id, adapter, descriptor = self._build_l2_adapter(config)
882	            for listener in self._registered_l2_listeners:
883	                adapter.register_listener(listener)
884	            with self._adapters_lock:
885	                self._l2_adapters[adapter_id] = adapter
886	                self._adapter_descriptors[adapter_id] = descriptor
887	            self._store_controller.add_adapter(adapter_id, adapter, descriptor)
888	            self._prefetch_controller.add_adapter(adapter_id, adapter, descriptor)
889	            if self._should_enable_l2_eviction(adapter, config.eviction_config):
890	                assert config.eviction_config is not None  # make linter happy
891	                self._l2_eviction_controller.add_adapter_state(
892	                    L2AdapterEvictionState(
893	                        adapter_id=adapter_id,
894	                        adapter=adapter,
895	                        eviction_config=config.eviction_config,
896	                    )
897	                )
898	            logger.info("Added L2 adapter %d (%s)", adapter_id, descriptor.type_name)
899	            return adapter_id
900	
901	    def delete_l2_adapter(self, adapter_id: int, timeout: float = 30.0) -> None:
902	        """Blocking function to drain the L2 adapter gracefully at runtime.
903	        Thread-safe.
904	
905	        Stops routing new stores/prefetches to the adapter, waits for its
906	        in-flight work to finish, removes it from the controllers, and
907	        closes it.
908	
909	        Args:
910	            adapter_id: Stable id of the adapter to remove.
911	            timeout: Maximum seconds to wait for in-flight work to drain.
912	
913	        Raises:
914	            ValueError: If no adapter with ``adapter_id`` is active.
915	            TimeoutError: If draining did not complete within ``timeout``;
916	                the adapter is left active (draining) so the caller can
917	                retry.
918	        """
919	        with self._lifecycle_lock:
920	            if adapter_id not in self._l2_adapters:
921	                raise ValueError(f"No L2 adapter with id {adapter_id}")
922	
923	            deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout
924	            store_done = self._store_controller.request_remove_adapter(adapter_id)
925	            prefetch_done = self._prefetch_controller.request_remove_adapter(adapter_id)
926	            if not store_done.wait(timeout=max(0.0, deadline - time.monotonic())):
927	                raise LMCacheTimeoutError(
928	                    f"Timed out draining adapter {adapter_id} from store controller"
929	                )
930	            if not prefetch_done.wait(timeout=max(0.0, deadline - time.monotonic())):
931	                raise LMCacheTimeoutError(
932	                    f"Timed out draining adapter {adapter_id} from prefetch controller"
933	                )
934	
935	            self._l2_eviction_controller.remove_adapter_state(adapter_id)
936	            with self._adapters_lock:
937	                adapter = self._l2_adapters.pop(adapter_id)
938	                self._adapter_descriptors.pop(adapter_id, None)
939	            adapter.close()
940	            logger.info("Deleted L2 adapter %d", adapter_id)
941	
942	    def l2_adapters(self) -> list[tuple[AdapterDescriptor, L2AdapterInterface]]:
943	        """Return all active L2 adapters paired with descriptors, in
944	        ascending adapter-id order (== configuration order for the initial
945	        set, then runtime-added adapters). The list is empty when no L2 is
946	        configured.
947	
948	        Do not cache the returned pairs — ``reconfigure_l2_adapter``,
949	        ``add_l2_adapter``, and ``delete_l2_adapter`` may change the set at
950	        runtime.
951	        """
952	        return [
953	            (desc, adapter) for _adapter_id, desc, adapter in self._snapshot_adapters()
954	        ]
955	
956	    # Management APIs
957	    def clear(self, force: bool = False):
958	        """
959	        Clear data in the storage manager.
960	
961	        Args:
962	            force: If True, clear ALL objects including locked ones.
963	                This may corrupt in-flight store/prefetch operations.
964	                If False (default), only clear unlocked objects, keeping
965	                write-locked and read-locked objects intact.
966	        """
967	        self._l1_manager.clear(force=force)
968	
969	    def close(self):
970	        """
971	        Close the storage manager and release all resources.
972	        """
973	        self._prefetch_controller.stop()
974	        self._store_controller.stop()
975	        self._eviction_controller.stop()
976	        self._l2_eviction_controller.stop()
977	
978	        PeriodicEventNotifier.shutdown()
979	
980	        for adapter in self._l2_adapters.values():
981	            adapter.close()
982	
983	        self._l1_manager.close()
984	
985	    def report_status(self) -> dict:
986	        """Return a status dict aggregating all sub-component statuses."""
987	        l1 = self._l1_manager.report_status()
988	        store = self._store_controller.report_status()
989	        prefetch = self._prefetch_controller.report_status()
990	        l1_eviction = self._eviction_controller.report_status()
991	        l2_eviction = self._l2_eviction_controller.report_status()
992	        adapters = [a.report_status() for _id, _desc, a in self._snapshot_adapters()]
993	        children = [l1, store, prefetch, l1_eviction, l2_eviction] + adapters
994	        return {
995	            "is_healthy": all(c["is_healthy"] for c in children),
996	            "l1_manager": l1,
997	            "store_controller": store,
998	            "prefetch_controller": prefetch,
999	            "l1_eviction_controller": l1_eviction,
1000	            "l2_eviction_controller": l2_eviction,
1001	            "l2_adapters": adapters,
1002	            "num_l2_adapters": len(adapters),
1003	        }
1004	
1005	    def register_l2_listener(self, listener: L2AdapterListener) -> None:
1006	        """Register a listener on all current and future L2 adapters.
1007	
1008	        The listener is recorded so that adapters added later via
1009	        :meth:`add_l2_adapter` receive it too.
1010	
1011	        Args:
1012	            listener: The listener to register.
1013	        """
1014	        with self._lifecycle_lock:
1015	            self._registered_l2_listeners.append(listener)
1016	            for adapter in self._l2_adapters.values():
1017	                adapter.register_listener(listener)
1018	
1019	    # Functions for debugging and testing
1020	    def memcheck(self) -> bool:
1021	        """
1022	        Perform memory check for all storage tiers.
1023	
1024	        Returns:
1025	            True if memory is consistent, False otherwise.
1026	        """
1027	        return self._l1_manager.memcheck()
1028	
1029	    def _snapshot_adapters(
1030	        self,
1031	    ) -> list[tuple[int, AdapterDescriptor, L2AdapterInterface]]:
1032	        """Snapshot the active adapters under the lock, in ascending
1033	        adapter-id order. Iterate this instead of the live dicts so a
1034	        concurrent add/delete cannot change them mid-iteration.
1035	
1036	        Returns:
1037	            A list of ``(adapter_id, descriptor, adapter)`` tuples.
1038	        """
1039	        with self._adapters_lock:
1040	            return [
1041	                (adapter_id, self._adapter_descriptors[adapter_id], adapter)
1042	                for adapter_id, adapter in sorted(self._l2_adapters.items())
1043	            ]
1044	
1045	    def _has_l2_adapters(self) -> bool:
1046	        """Return whether any L2 adapter is currently active."""
1047	        with self._adapters_lock:
1048	            return bool(self._l2_adapters)
1049	
1050	    def _build_l2_adapter(
1051	        self,
1052	        config: L2AdapterConfigBase,
1053	    ) -> tuple[int, L2AdapterInterface, AdapterDescriptor]:
1054	        """Create a L2 adapter instance based on the config.
1055	
1056	        Args:
1057	            config: The adapter configuration.
1058	
1059	        Returns:
1060	            A ``(adapter_id, adapter, descriptor)`` tuple. ``adapter_id`` is
1061	            the freshly allocated stable id, ``adapter`` is the new adapter
1062	            instance, and ``descriptor`` is its descriptor carrying that id.
1063	        """
1064	        adapter_id = self._next_adapter_id
1065	        self._next_adapter_id += 1
1066	        adapter: L2AdapterInterface = create_l2_adapter(config, self._l1_memory_desc)
1067	        if config.serde_config is not None:
1068	            adapter = SerdeL2AdapterWrapper(
1069	                inner=adapter,
1070	                serde=create_serde_processor(config.serde_config),
1071	                l1_manager=self._l1_manager,
1072	            )
1073	        descriptor = AdapterDescriptor(index=adapter_id, config=config)
1074	        return adapter_id, adapter, descriptor
1075	
1076	    def _should_enable_l2_eviction(
1077	        self,
1078	        adapter: L2AdapterInterface,
1079	        eviction_config: EvictionConfig | None,
1080	    ) -> bool:
1081	        """Whether to wire an adapter into the L2 eviction controller.
1082	
1083	        Args:
1084	            adapter: The adapter to evaluate.
1085	            eviction_config: The adapter's eviction config, if any.
1086	
1087	        Returns:
1088	            True if an eviction state should be created for this adapter.
1089	        """
1090	        if eviction_config is None:
1091	            return False
1092	        policy_name = eviction_config.eviction_policy
1093	        if policy_name != "IsolatedLRU" and not adapter.supports_global_eviction:
1094	            logger.warning(
1095	                "L2 adapter %s configured with '%s' eviction but does "
1096	                "not support global eviction (max_capacity_bytes=0); "
1097	                "skipping aggregate-usage eviction setup.",
1098	                type(adapter).__name__,
1099	                policy_name,
1100	            )
1101	            return False
1102	        return True
1103	
1104	    def _unwrap_reconfigurable_l2_adapter(
1105	        self,
1106	        adapter: L2AdapterInterface,
1107	    ) -> Optional[L2ReconfigurableAdapter]:
1108	        if isinstance(adapter, L2ReconfigurableAdapter):
1109	            return adapter
1110	
1111	        inner = getattr(adapter, "inner_adapter", None)
1112	        if inner is not None and isinstance(inner, L2ReconfigurableAdapter):
1113	            return inner
1114	
1115	        return None
1116	
1117	    def _list_reconfigurable_l2_adapters(
1118	        self,
1119	    ) -> list[tuple[int, L2ReconfigurableAdapter]]:
1120	        with self._adapters_lock:
1121	            items = sorted(self._l2_adapters.items())
1122	        adapters: list[tuple[int, L2ReconfigurableAdapter]] = []
1123	        for l2_adapter_index, adapter in items:
1124	            reconfigurable_adapter = self._unwrap_reconfigurable_l2_adapter(adapter)
1125	            if reconfigurable_adapter is not None:
1126	                adapters.append((l2_adapter_index, reconfigurable_adapter))
1127	        return adapters
1128	
1129	    def _get_reconfigurable_l2_adapter(
1130	        self,
1131	        adapter_index: int,
1132	    ) -> L2ReconfigurableAdapter:
1133	        adapters = self._list_reconfigurable_l2_adapters()
1134	        if adapter_index < 0 or adapter_index >= len(adapters):
1135	            raise L2ReconfigureError(404, "L2 adapter not reconfigurable")
1136	        return adapters[adapter_index][1]
